name: Botulinum toxin
description: Neurotoxin that blocks acetylcholine release at the neuromuscular junction, causing temporary muscle relaxation to reduce dynamic wrinkles.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for all skin types (I-VI). No melanin-targeting mechanism; does not cause PIH or hypopigmentation. Excellent safety profile across all Fitzpatrick types.
pre_care: Avoid blood thinners if medically appropriate
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to botulinum toxin or albumin
Concurrent aminoglycoside antibiotics or neuromuscular blockers

name: Dermal fillers
description: Injectable substances (HA, CaHA, PLLA, PMMA, autologous fat) that restore volume, smooth wrinkles, and enhance facial contours through physical expansion and/or biostimulation.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Generally safe for all skin types. Higher PIH risk in darker skin from injection trauma or vascular compromise. Tyndall effect (blue tint) more visible on lighter skin.

name: Biostimulators
description: Injectables that primarily induce endogenous collagen, elastin, and extracellular matrix production rather than providing immediate physical volume. Includes PLLA (Sculptra) and CaHA (Radiesse).
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for all skin types. PIH risk from injection trauma higher in darker skin. Use smallest gauge needles, minimize passes. Post-procedure sun protection critical for Fitzpatrick IV-VI.
pre_care: For PLLA: ensure proper reconstitution time
post_care: PLLA: 5-5-5 massage protocol (5 minutes, 5x daily, 5 days)
contraindications: Active infection or inflammation at site
For PLLA: recent radiation therapy to area
For CaHA: thin skin areas, lips

name: Laser resurfacing
description: Uses focused light energy to remove damaged skin layers and stimulate collagen remodeling. Includes ablative (CO2, Er:YAG) and non-ablative fractional systems.
fitzpatrick_considerations: I-II: excellent. III-IV: caution with ablative. V-VI: avoid ablative CO2; fractional CO2 high risk; Er:YAG best ablative option. Pre-treat with hydroquinone 4% + tretinoin for 4-6 weeks. PIH risk 10-30% with ablative in darker skin.
pre_care: Pre-treat darker skin with hydroquinone + tretinoin for 4-6 weeks
No sun exposure or tanning 2-4 weeks prior

name: IPL
description: Intense Pulsed Light uses broad spectrum light (500-1200 nm) filtered to target hemoglobin, melanin, and water for vascular lesions, pigment, photorejuvenation, and hair reduction.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Best for Fitzpatrick I-III. Caution for IV. Avoid/high risk for V-VI. Melanin absorbs broad IPL spectrum causing thermal injury, PIH, blistering. If used on darker skin: longer wavelengths (690+ nm), lower fluences, test spots mandatory.
pre_care: No sun exposure or tanning 2-4 weeks prior
post_care: Avoid heat and strenuous exercise 24-48 hours
Pigmented spots will darken before sloughing
contraindications: Active cold sore history (HSV prophylaxis needed)

name: Radiofrequency
description: Delivers electrical energy creating ohmic heating in tissues, causing immediate collagen contraction and long-term neocollagenesis. Includes monopolar (Thermage), bipolar, and fractional RF microneedling (Morpheus8).
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for ALL skin types (I-VI). Major advantage: no melanin chromophore dependence. Preferred energy device for skin of color. Fractional RF microneedling may cause temporary PIH in darker skin from needle trauma (rare).
pre_care: Discuss pain management options for monopolar
post_care: Gentle skincare; no heat exposure 24 hours

name: Ultrasound
description: High-intensity focused ultrasound (HIFU) delivers precise thermal energy to specific tissue depths (1.5-4.5 mm) including the SMAS layer for non-surgical skin lifting and tightening.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for ALL skin types (I-VI). No melanin dependence; ultrasound passes through melanin-rich epidermis without absorption. Excellent option for skin of color.
contraindications: Severe or cystic acne in treatment area

name: LED/Low-level light therapy
description: Uses specific light wavelengths (blue 415 nm, red 630-660 nm, NIR 830 nm) to trigger photochemical reactions in cells, increasing ATP production and modulating inflammation without thermal damage.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for ALL skin types (I-VI). No thermal injury; no risk of PIH or hypopigmentation. Ideal for sensitive skin and skin of color.
post_care: No restrictions; can resume all activities immediately
contraindications: Photosensitivity disorders (lupus, porphyria, albinism, xeroderma pigmentosum)
Active skin cancer or precancerous lesions

name: Chemical peels
description: Acidic solutions cause controlled epidermal and/or dermal injury, triggering wound healing and skin renewal. Depths: superficial (AHAs, BHA, Jessner's), medium (TCA), deep (phenol).
fitzpatrick_considerations: Superficial: good with caution for all. Medium TCA: high PIH risk in V-VI. Phenol: avoid V-VI. Pre-treat darker skin with hydroquinone 4% + tretinoin + sunscreen for 4-6 weeks. Mandelic acid is safest AHA for darker skin.
pre_care: Pre-treat darker skin with hydroquinone + tretinoin for 4-6 weeks
Discontinue retinoids 1 week prior (superficial) or longer (medium/deep)
post_care: Resume actives per provider guidance (1-4 weeks)

name: Microneedling
description: Collagen induction therapy using fine needles to create controlled micro-injuries, triggering wound healing cascade, growth factor release, and enhanced topical product delivery.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Generally safe for ALL skin types. Lower PIH risk than lasers because epidermis remains largely intact. Safer than lasers for darker skin when treating acne scars and pigment. Pre/post-treat with tyrosinase inhibitors for darker skin.

name: Microdermabrasion
description: Mechanical exfoliation using crystal or diamond-tip abrasion combined with vacuum suction to remove dead skin cells from the stratum corneum. Includes hydradermabrasion variants.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for ALL skin types. Very superficial so minimal PIH risk. Gentle approach recommended for Fitzpatrick IV-VI to avoid petechiae.
post_care: Enhanced product absorption — good time for serums

name: Dermarolling/Dermapen
description: At-home (0.2-0.5 mm) or professional (0.5-3.0 mm) microneedling using rollers or automated pen devices to create micro-channels for product penetration or collagen induction.
fitzpatrick_considerations: At-home: very safe for all skin types at cosmetic depths. Professional: safe for all skin types; preferred over lasers for darker skin. Caution with deeper needles in Fitzpatrick V-VI for transient PIH.
pre_care: At-home: sterilize device with rubbing alcohol
No active acne, eczema, or infections
post_care: At-home: minimal redness resolves in hours
Professional: redness 1-3 days, no makeup 24 hours
Apply hyaluronic acid or peptide serum
contraindications: Active infection, eczema, psoriasis, warts, moles

name: PRP/PRF
description: Platelet-Rich Plasma and Platelet-Rich Fibrin involve centrifuging patient's blood to concentrate platelets and growth factors, then injecting or topically applying to stimulate tissue repair and collagen.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for ALL skin types. Autologous (patient's own blood) so no allergic reaction. No thermal injury so no PIH risk. Excellent for skin of color.

name: Exosomes
description: Extracellular vesicles (30-150 nm) derived from stem cells or other sources that act as intercellular messengers, delivering proteins, lipids, and growth factors to modulate cellular behavior and stimulate repair.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Theoretical safety for all skin types. No thermal mechanism so no PIH risk. Long-term safety data lacking across all skin types. Monitor for adverse reactions.

name: Cryotherapy
description: Application of extreme cold (-100C to -196C) to destroy abnormal tissue or create vascular responses. Includes local cryosurgery, cryofacials, and whole-body cryotherapy chambers.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Local cryotherapy safe for all skin types. Post-inflammatory pigment changes (hypo- or hyperpigmentation) in darker skin after aggressive cryotherapy for lesions. Cryofacials generally safe for all.

name: HydraFacial
description: Patented multi-step facial using hydradermabrasion, gentle acid peel, vortex extraction, and serum infusion for deep cleansing, exfoliation, and hydration.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for ALL skin types. Gentle nature makes it suitable for sensitive and darker skin. No thermal injury or significant trauma.
pre_care: Avoid recent sun exposure or tanning
Discuss booster options for specific concerns

name: Photodynamic therapy
description: Uses photosensitizing agent (ALA or MAL) activated by light to create reactive oxygen species that destroy target cells. FDA-approved for actinic keratoses; used off-label for acne and photorejuvenation.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for most skin types. Challenge in darker skin: photosensitizer may cause temporary hyperpigmentation; light activation must be calibrated to avoid thermal injury to melanin-rich epidermis. Red light or blue light generally safer than IPL/PDL activation.
pre_care: Photosensitizer applied 1-3 hours before light activation
Avoid sun exposure day of treatment
HSV prophylaxis if history of cold sores
post_care: STRICT sun avoidance 48 hours (severe burns possible)
Use provided post-care kit if available

name: Thread lifts
description: Dissolvable or permanent sutures (PDO, PLLA, PCL) inserted to mechanically lift sagging tissue and stimulate collagen through foreign body response.
fitzpatrick_considerations: Safe for ALL skin types. No thermal injury so no PIH risk. Entry points may cause temporary PIH in darker skin. Excellent option for skin of color seeking lifting.
pre_care: Discuss realistic expectations (not a facelift substitute)

name: Microblading
description: Manual SPMU technique using handheld blade tool to create fine hair-like incisions in the epidermis/upper dermis, implanting pigment for natural brow appearance.
fitzpatrick_considerations: All skin types can undergo microblading. Challenges in darker skin: ash/gray undertones as pigment fades; PIH from procedure trauma (rare). Oily skin causes pigment to blur. Fitzpatrick V-VI: consider nano-blading or powder brows instead.
pre_care: No Botox in brow area 2 weeks prior
No chemical peels or laser on brows until healed
No waxing, threading, tweezing 2 weeks prior
post_care: Apply provided aftercare ointment thinly 2-3x daily
Avoid retinoids on tattooed area long-term
contraindications: Active skin infection, eczema, psoriasis in brow area
related_products: Brow growth serums (avoid on tattooed area)

name: Nano-bladed brows
description: Digital SPMU technique using a machine with single nano needle (0.18-0.25 mm) to create precise punctures rather than blade incisions, resulting in crisper, longer-lasting hair strokes with less trauma.
fitzpatrick_considerations: All skin types. Better option than microblading for Fitzpatrick IV-VI (less trauma, less risk of pigment ashing or PIH). Better for oily and sensitive skin. Still requires skilled color matching for darker skin.
pre_care: No Botox in brow area 2 weeks prior
No chemical peels or laser on brows
post_care: Similar to microblading but less scabbing
Avoid retinoids on tattooed area long-term
contraindications: Active infection, eczema, psoriasis in brow area

name: Powder/ombre brows
description: Digital SPMU technique using stippling/dotting motion to create a shaded, makeup-like brow appearance with gradient effect (lighter inner brow, darker tail).
fitzpatrick_considerations: All skin types. Better option for Fitzpatrick IV-VI than microblading. Shading technique less likely to cause ashy appearance. Color selection still critical. Works well for oily skin where microblading strokes blur.
pre_care: No Botox in brow area 2 weeks prior
No chemical peels or laser on brows
Discuss desired brow style (natural vs defined)
post_care: Avoid retinoids on tattooed area long-term
contraindications: Active infection, eczema, psoriasis in brow area

name: Eyeliner tattoo
description: Permanent pigment implanted along the lash line to enhance eye definition. Techniques include lash line enhancement (most natural), thin eyeliner, or winged eyeliner.
fitzpatrick_considerations: All skin types. Special considerations for darker skin: warm brown pigments often better than black (which can turn blue-gray). Blue-black pigments can turn blue-gray as they fade on all skin types.
pre_care: Discontinue eyelash growth serums (Latisse) 2+ weeks prior
No eye makeup day of procedure
Discuss desired style (lash enhancement vs winged)
contraindications: Recent eye surgery (LASIK, cataract — wait 6+ months)
Trigeminal neuralgia or facial pain syndromes

name: Lip blushing
description: Semi-permanent pigment implanted into lips to enhance color and definition. Techniques range from vermilion border definition to full lip color or ombre effects.
fitzpatrick_considerations: All skin types can undergo lip blushing. Critical for melanin-rich lips (Fitzpatrick IV-VI): may cause hyperpigmentation or uneven color; must neutralize dark tones first (orange/peach corrector); may not achieve desired pink color on very dark lips.
pre_care: HSV-1 prophylaxis: valacyclovir 500 mg BID starting 2 days before (MANDATORY if any cold sore history)
post_care: No spicy, salty, acidic foods 7-10 days
contraindications: History of cold sores (requires antiviral prophylaxis)
Active infection, eczema, psoriasis on lips

name: Freckle tattooing
description: Cosmetic tattooing to create artificial freckles using manual or machine application of pigment in dot pattern to mimic natural freckle distribution.
fitzpatrick_considerations: More visible and natural on Fitzpatrick I-III. On darker skin may appear as dark spots rather than freckles. Pigment migration risk higher on melanin-rich skin.
pre_care: Discuss realistic outcomes and fading patterns

name: Areola tattooing
description: Medical/paramedical tattooing to restore or enhance areola appearance post-mastectomy reconstruction, surgery, or for gender affirmation. Often creates 3D illusion of projection.
fitzpatrick_considerations: All skin types. Medical-grade pigments often more stable. Must match natural areola color for realistic appearance across all skin tones.
pre_care: Wait 3-6 months post-surgery for fully healed scars
No radiation therapy to area until complete

name: Scalp micropigmentation
description: Specialized cosmetic tattooing implanting tiny pigment dots at superficial depth to simulate hair follicles, creating illusion of closely-shaved hair or density in thinning areas.
fitzpatrick_considerations: All skin types. Color matching critical — must match hair follicle color for natural appearance. On darker skin use darker pigments; ensure artist experienced with skin of color.
pre_care: Shave head to desired length (if buzz-cut look)
Stabilize hair loss with medical treatments if possible
post_care: No swimming, heavy sweating, sun exposure 7-10 days
contraindications: Active scalp infection, psoriasis, seborrheic dermatitis
